zh.Netherfield, Sussex, England, United Kin: 英國英格蘭素可瑟費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Herbert Finch於大約1320年出生在英國素可瑟費爾德時，他的父親Vincent Herbert Finch II27歲，母親Joan de Salerne24歲。他於1355年與Parnell Allard在英國素可文爾希亞結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1385年在他的家鄉享年67歲。

zh-hans.Netherfield, Sussex, England, United Kin: 英国英格兰素可瑟費爾德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry Herbert Finch于大约1320年出生在英国素可瑟費爾德时，他的父亲Vincent Herbert Finch II27岁，母亲Joan de Salerne24岁。他于1355年与Parnell Allard在英国素可文爾希亞结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1385年在他的家乡享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English, and French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In Britain, this Old French name, introduced by the Normans, reinforced the less common Old English cognate Herebeorht. The surname was taken to Ireland after the Anglo-Norman invasion and in the 16th century.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the German personal name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Harbison
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name Herbert (the change from -er- to -ar- was a common one in Old French and Middle English). An intrusive n later gave Harbinson .

variants.name: Hebard
meanings: English (Surrey):
variant of Herbert , with assimilation of the first r. Compare Hebb .
variant of   Hibbert  .

variants.name: Harbert
meanings: German and English: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbers
meanings: German and Dutch: patronymic from   Herbert  . It is found mainly in Lower Saxony and across the Dutch border.

variants.name: Dickherber
meanings: Probably a nickname for a fat person by the name of Herbert , of which Herber is a short form, or, more likely, in the north from Die(c)kherber, a topographic and distinguishing name for a Herber living by the dike, see Dicke 2. Compare Dickmeyer .

variants.name: Hebert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Pierre, Lucien, Michel, Gaston, Alcide, Camille.
French (Hébert): assimilated form of Herbert . Compare Abair , Abar , Abare , Abear , Aber , Eber , and Ebert .
German: variant of   Heber   1.

variants.name: Herber
meanings: German and Dutch: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbertson
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name   Herbert  .

variants.name: Hebb
meanings: English (East Yorkshire and northeast Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Hebb, Ebb, pet forms of Herbert .

variants.name: Herb
meanings: South German: nickname for a harsh or rough person, from Middle High German herb ‘tart, harsh’.
German: from the personal name Herpo, a pet form of Herbert .

nameMeanings.name: Finch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English finch, fink ‘finch’ (Old English finc), for a small, lively, cheerful person.

Americanized form (translation into English) of German, Jewish (Ashkenazic), Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Fink ‘finch’, or of some of the corresponding Slavic surnames, e.g. Czech (Moravian) Pěnka (see Penka ).

variants.name: Fink
meanings: German, Jewish (Ashkenazic), Danish, and English: nickname for a lively and cheerful person, or, in the case of the Jewish name, an artificial name, from Middle High German vinke, Middle English and Yiddish fink ‘finch’ (see Finch and Vink ). The surname of German origin is also found in some central European countries, for example in Czechia, Slovenia, and Croatia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic nicknames or surnames. In part, this is a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname, originating from the Kočevsko region in Lower Carniola, Slovenia (see Kocevar ).

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Herbert Finch was born about 1320, in Netherfield, Sussex, England, his father, Vincent Herbert Finch II, was 27 and his mother, Joan de Salerne, was 24. He married Parnell Allard in 1355, in Winchelsea, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died about 1385, in his hometown, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry Herbert Finch est né environ 1320, à Netherfield, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Vincent Herbert Finch II, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Joan de Salerne, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Parnell Allard en 1355, à Winchelsea, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé environ 1385, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry Herbert Finch um 1320 in Netherfield, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Vincent Herbert Finch II 27 und seine Mutter Joan de Salerne 24. Er hat 1355 in Winchelsea, Sussex, England Parnell Allard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb um 1385 im Alter von 67 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Herbert Finch è nato il circa 1320 nel Netherfield, Sussex, England, il suo padre, Vincent Herbert Finch II, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Joan de Salerne, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Parnell Allard nel 1355, nel Winchelsea, Sussex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì circa 1385, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Herbert Finchが約1320年にイギリススセックスネスエルフィールドで生まれた時，父Vincent Herbert Finch IIは27歳，母Joan de Salerneは24歳であった。1355年にイギリススセックスウィンチェルセアでParnell Allardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は約1385年に故郷で67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Herbert Finch(이)가 대략 1320년 영국 수세스 네더필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Vincent Herbert Finch II(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Salerne(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1355년 영국 수세스 윈헬시에서 Parnell Allard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1385년 그의 고향에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Herbert Finch nasceu aproximadamente 1320, em Netherfield, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Vincent Herbert Finch II, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Salerne, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Parnell Allard em 1355, em Winchelsea, Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1385, em sua cidade natal, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Herbert Finch родился около 1320 г. в местности нетирфиелд, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Vincent Herbert Finch II было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Salerne, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Parnell Allard в 1355 г. в местности винчелсеа, суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер около 1385 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry Herbert Finch nació aproximadamente 1320, en Netherfield, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Vincent Herbert Finch II, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Joan de Salerne, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Parnell Allard en 1355, en Winchelsea, Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1385, en su pueblo, a la edad de 67 años.
